observe: More than one Core now. More lanes, more choices — and someone has to keep them balanced.
fault: One lane is buried and two are empty. Nobody moved the load.
repair: Spread it out. Balance the lanes before one of them breaks.
escape: Keep it balanced — an idle lane right now is wasted time.

observe: Multiple-processor scheduling requires balancing load across available CPUs.
fault: Load is unevenly distributed across processing cores.
repair: Distribute processes across the available cores.
escape: Complete the load-balanced run before time expires.

hints: Count total burst time per core, not just process count, when balancing.
